channel.title: Jake Richards MP for Rother Valley

item.title: Golders Green statemen
item.dc:creator: luke
item.description: I am deeply saddened by the horrific terrorist attack in Golders Green yesterday. My thoughts are with the victims and their families, I wish them well at this incredibly difficult time. The Government has announced an additional £25m towards Jewish community security following the attack and I know that police forces up and down the […]
item.content:encoded:
I am deeply saddened by the horrific terrorist attack in Golders Green yesterday. My thoughts are with the victims and their families, I wish them well at this incredibly difficult time.

The Government has announced an additional £25m towards Jewish community security following the attack and I know that police forces up and down the country are working hard to keep people safe on our streets.

But there is more we need to do. We need to call out antisemitism wherever we find see or experience it. This is for government, but incumbent on us all.

I want to be clear that Antisemitism has no place in Rother Valley, or anywhere across the country. It is the duty of all of us, as a community, to unite and stand firmly against hatred in every form.

No one should have to live in fear because of who they are.

item.title: International Workers’ Memorial Day
item.dc:creator: luke
item.description: Today is International Workers Memorial Day. Observed annually on April 28, it is an opportunity to remember workers killed, injured, or made ill by their work and to promote workplace safety worldwide. I remember in particular, the men and women who died in mining accidents, at Dinnington Main Colliery, with their names and memories captured […]
item.content:encoded:
Today is International Workers Memorial Day. Observed annually on April 28, it is an opportunity to remember workers killed, injured, or made ill by their work and to promote workplace safety worldwide.

I remember in particular, the men and women who died in mining accidents, at Dinnington Main Colliery, with their names and memories captured here on the beautiful, yet poignant remembrance wall.

Their legacy will forever be honoured within the community and serve as a reminder of the importance of workplace safety today.

item.title: Football sessions at Dinnington High School
item.dc:creator: luke
item.description: Wonderful to visit Dinnington High School last week, to meet with staff from the school and from the Coalfield Regeneration Trust, who are funding free after school football sessions for pupils, as part of their Game On initiative. It was great to see the pupils so involved in the programme (despite the bad weather on […]
item.content:encoded:
Wonderful to visit Dinnington High School last week, to meet with staff from the school and from the Coalfield Regeneration Trust, who are funding free after school football sessions for pupils, as part of their Game On initiative.

It was great to see the pupils so involved in the programme (despite the bad weather on Friday), enjoying a two hour session after school. Turnout was great and they are hoping to see the games grow in the coming weeks and months.

Thanks for inviting me along!
